Section 7 (Conclusions and discussions), page 24 (Question 7.1); see also Appendix A discussion around pages 28–29.

Question 7.1. When (A.8) is infeasible, what is a computationally convenient certificate for unboundedness of (A.1)?
title: Certificate for unboundedness of polynomial optimization when moment-ray condition fails
background_latex:
Let \(K\subseteq \mathbb{R}^n\) be a basic closed semialgebraic set
\[
K:=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: c_i(x)=0\ (i\in E),\ c_j(x)\ge 0\ (j\in I)\},
\]
where \(E,I\) are finite index sets and each \(c_i,c_j\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) is a polynomial. Let \(g\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) be a polynomial of degree \(d\), and consider the polynomial optimization problem
\[
\inf\{g(x): x\in K\}.
\]
Write \(\tilde x:=(x_0,x)\in\mathbb{R}^{n+1}\). The homogenization of a polynomial \(p\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) of degree \(\deg(p)\) is
\(\tilde p(\tilde x):=x_0^{\deg(p)}p(x/x_0)\), and its highest-degree homogeneous part is \(p_{\mathrm{hom}}(x):=\tilde p(0,x)\).
Define the compact “homogenized” feasible set
\[
\tilde K:=\{\tilde x:\ \tilde c_i(\tilde x)=0\ (i\in E),\ \tilde c_j(\tilde x)\ge 0\ (j\in I),\ \|\tilde x\|_2^2=1,\ x_0\ge 0\},
\]
and the “horizon” (the section \(x_0=0\))
\[
K^{\circ}:=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: c_{i,\mathrm{hom}}(x)=0\ (i\in E),\ c_{j,\mathrm{hom}}(x)\ge 0\ (j\in I),\ \|x\|_2^2=1\}.
\]
A (truncated) moment sequence \(z\) of degree \(d\) supported on \(K^{\circ}\) is an element of the moment cone \(R_d(K^{\circ})\), i.e., there exists a Borel measure \(\mu\) supported on \(K^{\circ}\) such that \(z_\alpha=\int x^\alpha\,d\mu\) for all \(|\alpha|\le d\). For a polynomial \(p\in\mathbb{R}[x]_d\), the pairing is \(\langle p,z\rangle:=\sum_{|\alpha|\le d} p_\alpha z_\alpha\).
The paper discusses a sufficient “decreasing-ray” moment certificate for unboundedness: feasibility of a linear moment system of the form
\[
\langle g_{\mathrm{hom}}, z\rangle=-1,\quad z\in R_d(K^{\circ}).
\]
(Equivalently, the existence of a ray in an appropriate dual moment program.) This condition is sufficient for \(\inf_{x\in K} g(x)=-\infty\), but the paper exhibits examples where \(\inf_{x\in K} g(x)=-\infty\) while this moment system is infeasible.
problem_definition_latex:
Given polynomials \(g\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) and \(c_i,c_j\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) defining \(K\subseteq\mathbb{R}^n\) as above, design a computationally convenient certificate (a verifiable condition) that implies
\[
\inf\{g(x):x\in K\}=-\infty,
\]
in the regime where the moment-ray condition
\[
\langle g_{\mathrm{hom}}, z\rangle=-1,\quad z\in R_d(K^{\circ})
\]
is infeasible.

Equivalently: characterize or certify unboundedness of \(\inf_{x\in K} g(x)\) by an efficiently checkable condition that applies even when no feasible \(z\in R_d(K^{\circ})\) satisfies \(\langle g_{\mathrm{hom}}, z\rangle=-1\).
latex:
Let \(K\subseteq \mathbb{R}^n\) be a basic closed semialgebraic set
\[
K:=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: c_i(x)=0\ (i\in E),\ c_j(x)\ge 0\ (j\in I)\},
\]
where \(E,I\) are finite index sets and each \(c_i,c_j\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) is a polynomial. Let \(g\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) be a polynomial of degree \(d\), and consider the polynomial optimization problem
\[

\]
Write \(\tilde x:=(x_0,x)\in\mathbb{R}^{n+1}\). The homogenization of a polynomial \(p\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) of degree \(\deg(p)\) is

Define the compact “homogenized” feasible set
\[

and the “horizon” (the section \(x_0=0\))
\[

\[

Given polynomials \(g\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) and \(c_i,c_j\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) defining \(K\subseteq\mathbb{R}^n\) as above, design a computationally convenient certificate (a verifiable condition) that implies
\[

\[
\langle g_{\mathrm{hom}}, z\rangle=-1,\quad z\in R_d(K^{\circ})

location_in_paper: Section 7 (Conclusions and discussions), page 24 (Question 7.1); see also Appendix A discussion around pages 28–29.
open_problem_quote: Question 7.1. When (A.8) is infeasible, what is a computationally convenient certificate for unboundedness of (A.1)?
counterexample_suitability_rationale: It is feasible to search computationally for instances where a proposed certificate fails or is incomplete, because unboundedness can often be numerically suggested by finding descent rays/sequences and checking SOS/moment relaxations. However, the space of semialgebraic sets and polynomials is vast, and verifying true unboundedness (as opposed to numerical evidence) is nontrivial.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: An evolutionary coding loop could explore candidate certificate templates (e.g., alternative homogenizations, SOS/Positivstellensatz forms, auxiliary variables) and test them on automatically generated benchmark families using SDP relaxations as a fitness proxy. But turning empirical success into a general, correct certificate likely requires substantial theoretical insight beyond what automated search can ensure.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper (Nie–Yang) reduces unboundedness of \(\inf\{g(x):x\in K\}\) to infeasibility of a conic lower-bounding problem over \(P_d(K)\), and via homogenization to a dual moment problem over the compactified set \(\tilde K\). A sufficient certificate is the existence of a “decreasing ray” supported on the horizon \(K^{\circ}\): find \(z\in R_d(K^{\circ})\) with \(\langle g_{\mathrm{hom}},z\rangle=-1\). The open issue is that this horizon-moment system can be infeasible even when the original problem is unbounded below; this reflects subtle gaps between behavior at infinity and representability/closedness properties of the relevant truncated moment cones.

Subsequent work on noncompact polynomial optimization provides partial progress toward alternative, computationally checkable certificates. In particular, homogenization-based frameworks for unbounded sets develop convergent SDP hierarchies that more systematically encode asymptotic behavior, potentially capturing unboundedness even when a single truncated horizon moment condition fails. Complementarily, positivity certificates tailored to non-compact semialgebraic sets (using modified quadratic modules/preorderings and growth conditions) offer SOS-verifiable sufficient conditions that can certify (un)boundedness without relying solely on a single horizon moment ray. Generalized truncated moment problem results on unbounded sets clarify when truncated moment data fail to admit representing measures and suggest that higher-order extensions or alternative cones may be necessary.

Overall, there is no generally accepted necessary-and-sufficient, efficiently checkable certificate that covers the failure regime of \(\langle g_{\mathrm{hom}},z\rangle=-1\) with \(z\in R_d(K^{\circ})\). The most promising directions combine (i) refined homogenization/compactification plus SDP hierarchies that incorporate additional asymptotic constraints beyond \(K^{\circ}\), and (ii) noncompact Positivstellensatz-type certificates that enforce coercivity or detect directions of escape through SOS conditions. Bridging the remaining gap likely requires characterizing when unboundedness is witnessed only by non-measure-theoretic objects at the truncation level (e.g., limits of feasible moment sequences without a representing measure) and designing relaxations that can detect such limiting behavior.

sources.title: Global optimization with polynomials and the problem of moments
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces the Moment-SOS (Lasserre) hierarchy for polynomial optimization via moment and SOS relaxations, providing convergence under archimedean/compactness assumptions.
sources.connection: Provides the foundational moment/SOS framework used in the paper’s decreasing-ray certificate; however it does not address noncompact unboundedness certificates when the horizon-moment system is infeasible.

sources.title: Discriminants and nonnegative polynomials
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops homogenization-at-infinity techniques and discriminant-based analysis for nonnegativity and polynomial optimization, including the notion of sets closed at infinity.
sources.connection: The open problem is explicitly about certificates for unboundedness on noncompact semialgebraic sets; this paper underpins the homogenization/horizon set methodology used to derive the moment-ray condition and its limitations.

sources.title: Linear optimization with cones of moments and nonnegative polynomials
sources.theorem_or_result: Studies linear conic optimization over moment cones and nonnegative polynomial cones, giving SDP hierarchies and convergence results for generalized moment problems on compact sets.
sources.connection: The decreasing-ray condition is a linear moment feasibility problem; this work provides the general conic duality and SDP approximation viewpoint, but compactness assumptions limit direct applicability to the failure regime on horizons/nonclosed cones.

sources.title: Positivity certificates and polynomial optimization on non-compact semialgebraic sets
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops positivity certificates and convergent SDP hierarchies for polynomial optimization on noncompact semialgebraic sets using alternative quadratic modules/preorderings and growth/coercivity-type conditions.
sources.connection: Directly targets the noncompact setting where standard moment/SOS methods and simple horizon-ray certificates can fail; offers computationally checkable sufficient conditions (via SOS certificates) for boundedness/unboundedness behavior beyond the basic horizon moment system.

sources.title: Homogenization for polynomial optimization with unbounded sets
sources.theorem_or_result: Provides a homogenization framework and SDP hierarchies for polynomial optimization over unbounded semialgebraic sets, with conditions ensuring convergence and mechanisms to handle behavior at infinity.
sources.connection: Most directly connected: it refines the homogenization-at-infinity approach used in the source paper and can yield alternative certificates/algorithms for detecting unboundedness when simple horizon moment-ray feasibility fails.

sources.title: Generalized truncated moment problems with unbounded sets
sources.theorem_or_result: Extends truncated moment problem theory and SDP methods to unbounded sets, addressing existence/approximation of representing measures and convergence issues in noncompact settings.
sources.connection: The failure of the moment-ray condition is tied to nonclosedness/representation issues for moment cones on horizon sets; generalized truncated moment results help explain and potentially remedy infeasibility gaps via higher-degree extensions or alternative cones.

sources.title: Tight relaxations for polynomial optimization and Lagrange multiplier expressions
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces tight SDP relaxations using KKT/Lagrange multiplier polynomial expressions, often achieving finite convergence without archimedeanness under genericity conditions.
sources.connection: While not an unboundedness certificate per se, tight relaxations can help detect infeasibility/boundedness by solving modified KKT systems and may complement homogenization-based unboundedness detection when horizon moment rays are absent.
